definitions.definition: It refers to the improvement in the well-being of individuals and society, focusing on the aspects of health, education, and income.
A composite indicator used to rank countries based on their human development achievements.
The average number of years a person is expected to live at birth, based on the mortality rates prevailing in a given year.
The average number of years a person is expected to live at age 1, based on the mortality rates prevailing in a given year.
The total value of goods and services produced by a country, including income earned by its citizens from abroad.
The percentage of a country's population that can read and write.
The minimum level of income required for a person to meet their basic needs and lead a dignified life.
A situation where people lack basic capabilities, such as education, health, and living standards, to lead a dignified life.
A composite indicator used to measure the deprivation of a population in terms of life expectancy, education, and income.
A development approach that ensures the participation and benefits of all sections of society, particularly the disadvantaged groups.
A development approach that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.
A model that explains the changes in the population growth rate of a country, from high birth and death rates to low birth and death rates.
A graphical representation of the age and sex structure of a population, showing the distribution of people across different age groups.
The average number of children born to a woman in her lifetime, based on the current age-specific fertility rates.
The proportion of dependent population (children and elderly) to the working-age population in a country.
The population aged 60 years and above, who are often dependent on others for their care and support.
A periodic count of the population of a country, conducted by the government to collect data on demographic characteristics.
